Validate contract entry sizes in extend/restore footprint ops

Add validate_contract_ledger_entry() check to both extend_footprint_ttl and
restore_footprint operations, mirroring stellar-core's validateContractLedgerEntry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add tree search, URL deep linking, clipboard copy, and improved X++ syntax highlighting (#40)

* Add tree search and URL deep linking features

Tree Search:
- Search button opens a dialog to enter search text
- Recursively walks the tree to find matching nodes (case-insensitive)
- Next/Prev buttons navigate between matches with wrap-around
- Search state clears when switching to a different object

URL Deep Linking:
- New ARBAOTBrowserUrlHelper class builds shareable URLs
- init() parses ObjectType, ObjectName, TreePath query parameters
- Copy Link button in action pane shows the URL in the infolog
- URL format: ?mi=ARBAOTBrowser&ObjectType=Table&ObjectName=CustTable

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement clipboard copy via extensible control

Replace info()-based Copy Link with a proper browser clipboard copy
using a JavaScript extensible control pattern:
- ARBClipboardHelperBuild: design-time build class
- ARBClipboardHelperControl: runtime control with FormProperty
- ARBClipboardHelper form: transient dialog hosting the control
- ARBClipboardHelper class: static copyToClipboard() entry point
- HTML/JS resources using navigator.clipboard.writeText()

The dialog opens briefly when Copy Link is clicked, copies the URL
to the browser clipboard via JavaScript, then auto-closes after 2s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Deploy web resources to webroot in Mount.ps1

Extensible control resources (HTML/JS) must be copied to the IIS
webroot/resources/ folder for the browser to load them. Add an
automatic deployment step to Mount.ps1 that copies resource content
from AxResource/ResourceContent/ to the webroot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix deep linking: use getClientURLQueryValue instead of HttpContext

HttpContext::Current is null during form init() in D365FO's SPA
architecture. Replace with Global::getClientURLQueryValue() which
uses SessionContext to access the original browser URL parameters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Include tree path in Copy Link URL for node-level deep linking

CopyLinkButton now captures the currently selected tree node path
via ARBAOTBrowserUrlHelper::getSelectedTreePath() and includes it
as a TreePath parameter in the URL. When opened, the form navigates
directly to that tree node.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor tree search to Docentric inline pattern

Replace dialog-based tree search with inline filter group using the
Docentric pattern (DocReportUserDefinedPlaceholderExpressionDesigner).

Changes:
- Add TreeSearchFilterGroup with StringControl + Prev/Next buttons
  inside DetailsNavigationList, above the ObjectTree
- TreeSearchButton in action pane now toggles filter group visibility
- Pre-build ascending/descending search index lists after tree
  construction (buildSearchTreeItems/buildSearchTreeItemsRecursive)
- Use match() for wildcard-aware search via findTreeItem()
- Remove old dialog-based methods (treeSearchExecute, treeSearchClear,
  treeSearchCollectMatches, treeSearchNavigate)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve X++ syntax highlighting in code viewer

- Replace C#-centric keyword list with accurate X++ keywords
  - Add: final, implements, retry, next, firstonly, forupdate, nofetch,
    forceselectorder, crosscompany, display, edit, server, client,
    ttsbegin/ttscommit/ttsabort, changecompany, mod, div, and more

- Separate operators from punctuation for distinct visual styling
- Add macro usage (#MacroName) highlighting as Comment.Preproc
- Add additional preprocessor directives (globaldefine, defjob, etc.)

- event, eventhandler (event handling)

- optimisticlock, pessimisticlock, repeatableread (concurrency)
- print (debug output)
- reverse (select ordering)
- set, unchecked (misc keywords)

* Add MotorController module with IMU-aided driving

New motor_controller.py module providing high-level async motor control:
- Accelerometer-based distance driving (forward_mm/backward_mm) using
  double-integration with proportional deceleration for accurate stopping
- Gyro-based precise turning (turn/turn_left/turn_right) using IMU feedback
- Time-based driving and turning (forward/backward/timed_turn) preserved
- Acceleration ramps for smooth start/stop
- front_face orientation support: rotates accelerometer X/Y axes to match
  actual robot forward direction regardless of HexDrive mounting position
- Diagnostic output ([MC-DIAG]) for calibration and debugging
- run_instructions() translates button sequences respecting drive_mode setting

app.py changes:
- Import and create MotorController when HexDrive is detected
- Wire async instruction execution via _run_instructions_async()
- Add settings: drive_mode (Time/Distance), drive_step_mm, accel_scale

constants.py changes:
- Add drive mode, distance step, and accelerometer tuning constants

Note: Gyro-based turning has not yet been tuned/calibrated to the same
degree as accelerometer distance driving. Distance mode achieves ~98mm
accuracy on 100mm targets after calibration.

* start of PID steering algorithm (P only)

* Upgrade line follower differential steering from P-only to full PID controller (#73)

* Initial plan

* Enhance line follower differential steering to use full PID controller

- Replace FOLLOWER_PROPORTIONAL_SCALE_FACTOR with PID gain defaults (Kp, Ki, Kd)
- Add pid_kp, pid_ki, pid_kd to _settings system for runtime adjustability
- Add PID state variables (_pid_integral, _pid_previous_error) in __init__
- Extract differential output computation into _compute_differential_output method
- Implement full PID algorithm with proportional, integral, and derivative terms
- Add diagnostic logging output showing error, P/I/D terms, correction and output
- Reset PID state when exiting follower mode

* chore: fix link-check false positives in lychee config

The link-check CI was failing on three classes of false positive:

1. LinkedIn profile URLs (status 999) — LinkedIn returns 999 to all
   automated crawlers as an anti-scraping measure. Profile URLs are
   valid and reachable in a browser. Added regex exclude for
   linkedin.com/in/* paths.

2. Forbes article URLs (status 403) — Forbes blocks automated requests.
   The referenced articles exist. Added exclude for forbes.com.

3. EU AI Act on eur-lex.europa.eu (status 202) — the server returns
   "202 Accepted" instead of "200 OK". Non-standard but the page loads
   fine. Added 202 to the accept list alongside 200, 206, and 429.

commit.message:
fix(bench): remove per-iteration deopt reset that caused 250x regression

The per-iteration ba.reset_maglev_deopt_count() inside b.iter() forced
Maglev to retry on every Criterion iteration even when it consistently
deopts. For deep_object this caused tried=3312 deopted=3312 (all
overflow), adding ~0.5us deopt overhead per iteration (796us total).
Without the reset, Maglev's exponential backoff kicks in after warmup
deopts, letting the interpreter run at full speed (~3us for deep_object).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reject unsolicited tx sets and refresh cache on access

Only cache tx sets that were actually pending (requested) — unsolicited
tx sets from the network are now rejected by receive() to prevent cache
poisoning. Additionally, get() now refreshes cached_at so actively-used
entries survive eviction under cache pressure.

Fixes #1179

perf(maglev): extend i32_range to Generic bitwise ops + optimistic Phi seeding

Three codegen analysis improvements:

1. GenericBitwiseOr/And/Xor/Not are unconditionally i32_range producers
   (JS bitwise ops always call ToInt32 per spec). This enables:
   - sum Phi in (sum + i*3) | 0 pattern → i32_range → smi_guarded
   - 32-bit ADD/MUL fast paths instead of 64-bit Smi checks

2. Optimistic Phi seeding in compute_i32_range for loop-counter patterns.
   Phis with at least one i32_range input are optimistically seeded,
   then confirmed by fixed-point (GenericIncrement of i32 Phi → i32).
   This breaks the circular dependency for loop counters like
   for (var i = 0; i < N; i++) where i Phi(0, Inc(i)) is circular.

3. BitwiseOr identity (x | 0) optimization for non-smi-guarded path.
   When right operand is constant 0, skip the right Smi check and OR
   instruction entirely — just Smi-check left and copy.

Expected impact: arithmetic_loop should improve from ~2x to near V8
parity (LEA for *3, skip MOVSXD, fewer Smi checks per iteration).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix format string error and add run duration output

PowerShell's -f operator has higher precedence than +, so building a
format string via string concatenation like '  {0,-' + \ + '}...' -f
caused the last segment '}  {2}' to be evaluated as a format string first,
making the leading } an unmatched closing brace (error near offset 1).

Fix: pre-build all format strings using double-quoted interpolation so
\/\ are resolved at string-creation time, before any -f operator.

Also add run duration message at the end of both scripts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
